SIG-INTENT-BROKEN-LINKS-FIXED-2026-05-20 — Cross-Reference Repairs
Decision: Name the product "Intent"
Decision: Methodology first, tool second
Decision: Open development — signals, decisions, architecture all public
Decision: File-native, git-tracked, OTel-compatible — no lock-in
Decision: Target practitioner-architects first
Decision: Specs as contracts, not stories
Decision: Staged GTM — thought leadership → methodology → tooling (conditional)
Decision: Four-product framing — Notice, Spec, Execute, Observe are distinct products
Decision: Three-layer architecture — compiled knowledge base + transformation OS + spec/code
Decision: Compilation over retrieval — knowledge base compiles understanding once
Decision: Double-loop learning — Observe updates domain understanding, not just execution
Decision: Origin tracking on all knowledge artifacts — human | agent | synthetic
Decision: Federated knowledge base — Core is universal substrate, engagements are bounded instances
Decision: Two products, not one — Intent (methodology) and Knowledge Engine (product) are distinct
Decision: Engagement rollout order — Subaru → F&G → ASA → Cargill → Footlocker
Decision: Knowledge Engine as new MCP server — intent-knowledge on port 8004
Decision: Retroactive enrichment is suggested, not automatic cascade
Decision: Redaction at tool level — MCP server applies confidentiality projection automatically
Decision: Spec shaping is self-prompting through four personas
Decision: ULID-based ID generation for all entity IDs
Decision: 12-factor agent pattern integration — 5 gaps resolved, event catalog expanded
